WARNING: These test points are for A2338 M2 (820-02773) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files.
title: Common Faults — A2338 M2 MacBook Pro 13"
description: Known common failure modes for the A2338 M2 (820-02773) logic board, compiled from repair experience and schematic analysis. Board features: 2x USB-C, DFR (Touch Bar), fan, Rasputin WiFi module.

faults.symptom: No power — no current draw on DCPS

common_causes.cause: CD3217 (UF400/UF500) failure — no VBUS pass-through
common_causes.check: Measure PPVBUS_USBC0 at CF400. If 0V with cable connected, UF400 failed.
common_causes.fix: Replace UF400 (CD3217). Check for liquid damage on USB-C connector pins.

common_causes.cause: USB-C connector damage (bent CC pins)
common_causes.check: Visual inspection of USB-C connector. Check CC1/CC2 continuity.
common_causes.fix: Replace USB-C connector. Clean corrosion if liquid damaged.

common_causes.cause: SYS_DETECT circuit open — battery not detected
common_causes.check: Probe TP5100/TP5101 (page 24). Should go high when battery flex connected.
common_causes.fix: Check J5150 BMU connector pins. Clean or replace. Check R5155.

faults.symptom: Low current draw (0.05-0.2A at 20V) — no boot

common_causes.cause: PP3V8_AON not coming up — SVR AON enable problem
common_causes.check: Measure PP3V8_AON. Check PPVAON_PWR_EN (U5349 delay circuit, page 26).
common_causes.fix: Check D5342/U5349 delay circuit components. Check CHGR_EN_RVR signal.

common_causes.cause: MPMU (U8100) not starting — PMU_RESET_L stuck low
common_causes.check: Check PMU_RESET_L signal (page 39). Check charger reset circuit U8740/U8744 (page 41).
common_causes.fix: If reset stuck low, check U8740 charger reset circuit. May need U8100 replacement.

faults.symptom: High current draw (>1.5A at 5V) — short circuit

common_causes.cause: PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN short — most common short on M2 boards
common_causes.check: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN capacitors. Should be >0.4V. If <0.1V, shorted.
common_causes.fix: Thermal camera to find hot chip. Remove shorted component (often a decoupling cap or SoC BGA issue).

common_causes.cause: PPBUS_AON short — capacitor or charger IC failure
common_causes.check: Diode mode on PBUS caps (C5800-C5807, page 27). Check DCIN caps (C5450-C5452).
common_causes.fix: Remove shorted cap. If still shorted after cap removal, charger IC U5200 may have internal short.

common_causes.cause: PP3V8_AON_WLBT short — WiFi module failure
common_causes.check: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON_WLBT net. Disconnect WiFi module to isolate.
common_causes.fix: Replace Rasputin WiFi module if shorted.

faults.symptom: Board boots but no display (backlight or image)

common_causes.cause: No PPBUS_AONSW_LCD — backlight boost has no input
common_causes.check: Measure PPBUS_AONSW_LCD at backlight circuit input.
common_causes.fix: Check load switch enable signal. Check for liquid damage on load switch components.

common_causes.cause: Display connector JP600 damage
common_causes.check: Inspect JP600 (SLG5586006) connector and associated passives. Check PP5V_SM_LCD.
common_causes.fix: Clean/reflow JP600. Replace if pins are damaged.

common_causes.cause: LCD cable or panel failure
common_causes.check: Try known-good LCD. Check LDP0_INT data lanes at connector.
common_causes.fix: Replace LCD panel or cable.

faults.symptom: No DFR (Touch Bar) — keyboard may or may not work

common_causes.cause: DFR LDO failure — PP1V8_DFR or PP3V3_DFR missing
common_causes.check: Measure PP1V8_DFR at UT700 output and PP3V3_DFR at UT710 output.
common_causes.fix: Replace UT700 or UT710. Check DFR_PWR_EN enable signal.

common_causes.cause: DFR flex connector damage (JT700 touch or JT710 display)
common_causes.check: Inspect DFR connectors for corrosion or mechanical damage.
common_causes.fix: Clean or replace DFR flex connector.

faults.symptom: Liquid damage — battery distribution/charging circuit failure

common_causes.cause: Charger IC U5200 corrosion — PPBUS_AON unstable or missing
common_causes.check: Inspect U5200 area for corrosion. Measure PPBUS_AON stability.
common_causes.fix: Clean board thoroughly. Replace U5200 if corroded. Rebuild surrounding passives (R5275, C5200 area).
common_causes.note: From Safan diagnostic records: multiple A2338 liquid damage cases requiring Tier 2 logic board repair (battery distribution/charging circuit rebuild). Typical quote: £329-£399.

common_causes.cause: CHGR_AUX_OK circuit corrosion (page 26)
common_causes.check: Check W5330 pull-up and delay circuit (D5342/D5349/U5349).
common_causes.fix: Clean and replace corroded passives in charger support area.

common_causes.cause: Keyboard connector area corrosion
common_causes.check: Inspect keyboard flex connector and surrounding passives.
common_causes.fix: Clean thoroughly. Replace damaged components. Check PP1V8_AONSW_KBDLO rail.

common_causes.cause: Rasputin WiFi module failure or PP3V8_AON_WLBT short
common_causes.check: Measure PP3V8_AON_WLBT. Check PP3V3_S2_WLBT_1MS (1.25V and 3.3V WiFi rails).
common_causes.fix: If power rails present but no WiFi: Rasputin module dead. If rails shorted: remove module.

common_causes.cause: WLBT_PWR_EN not asserted
common_causes.check: Check WLBT_PWR_EN signal from MPMU GPIO.
common_causes.fix: Check MPMU GPIO configuration. May indicate broader PMU issue.

common_causes.cause: Antenna connector disconnected or damaged
common_causes.check: Check UL000 antenna connectors (page 74). Ensure all 3 antenna leads connected.
common_causes.fix: Reconnect or replace antenna cables.

faults.symptom: Fan running at full speed / overheating

common_causes.cause: Thermal sensor open circuit — SMC defaults to max fan
common_causes.check: Check MPMU TDEV sensors (page 58). Check for open thermistors (RE111-RE171).
common_causes.fix: Replace open thermistor. Common: TSCD (SoC backside) or TPMP (PMU proximity).

common_causes.cause: Fan connector damage — no tachometer feedback
common_causes.check: Check FAN_RT_TACH signal. Check fan connector pins.
common_causes.fix: Clean fan connector. Replace fan if tachometer failure.

faults.symptom: USB-C ports not working (no data/charging on one or both)

common_causes.cause: CD3217 port controller failure (UF400 or UF500)
common_causes.check: Check PP1V05_UPC0_LDO (internal LDO) at UF400. Check PP5V_S2_UPC from UF800.
common_causes.fix: Replace UF400 or UF500 (CD3217). Reprogram SPI ROM if needed.

common_causes.cause: ATC retimer regulator failure
common_causes.check: Measure PP1V2_USBC_VDDIO (UF900), PPVD055_USBC_CIO (UF939).
common_causes.fix: Replace failed ATC regulator (UF900/UF910/UF920/UF930/UF939/UF960).

common_causes.cause: USB2 repeater failure (UF600/UF605)
common_causes.check: Check PP3V25_S2_100 power to UF600. Check eUSB signals.
common_causes.fix: Replace UF600 or UF605.

faults.symptom: No audio — speakers or headphone jack

common_causes.cause: Audio codec UE500 (Carlow B0) failure
common_causes.check: Check PP3V8_AON and PP1V25_MAME_ID power to UE500. Check I2C communication.
common_causes.fix: Replace UE500 audio codec.

common_causes.cause: Speaker amplifier failure (URS00/URS30/URS60)
common_causes.check: Check PP3V8_MAME and PP1V25_MAME_ID to amplifiers. Check TDM signals.
common_causes.fix: Replace failed amplifier IC.

notes: A2338 is used for BOTH M1 and M2 MacBook Pro 13". This file covers the M2 variant (820-02773). The M1 variant uses a different schematic (820-02020).
DFR (Touch Bar) is unique to this model — not present on MacBook Air or later MacBook Pro models.
Rasputin WiFi module (WiFi 6E) replaces the Typhoon module used in A2681 MacBook Air M2.
Battery flex is SOLDERED to MLB (J5150A solder pads, page 24) — requires desoldering for replacement.
